Records.description: Divergent one-loop triangle I_3^D(0,p_2^2,m^2;0,0,m^2) with one massive internal line, p_1^2=0 and p_3^2=m^2. Contains double 1/eps^2 pole from soft singularity.

Records.authors: Bjerrum-Bohr, N.E. J., Damgaard, Poul H., Festuccia, Guido, Planté, Ludovic, Vanhove, Pierre
Records.description: One-loop scalar triangle integral with one massive propagator (mass m1=Sqrt[p1^2]) and two massless propagators, finite in d=4. Defined in the main text (line 168) as the building block for the classical part of the one-loop two-graviton scattering amplitude M2 in Eq. (e:one). Fully evaluated in closed form in Appendix A using a Feynman parametric representation: integration over x1 yields a logarithmic integrand, and integration over x2 gives a result expressed via dilogarithms Li_2 and logarithms involving the variables rho_pm = -1/2 +- (1/2)*Sqrt[1 - 4*p1^2/q^2]. The large-mass limit |q^2|/|p1^2| << 1 gives I = (i/(32*q^2))*Sqrt[-q^2/p1^2] + O(q^2/p1^2), reproducing the leading classical contribution to general relativity through Eq. (e:Triresult).
Records.location: Eq. between (1)-(2) at line 168 (main text) and Appendix A (Eqs. (A.1)-(A.5))
